mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 10 percent, severe labor market deterioration signals a deep recession that crushes corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 10 percent, invalidating the recession-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis liquidity injection phase confirms supply chain normalization and manufacturing restart, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns during liquidity expansion periods, with a mean return difference indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly expands corporate profit pools and economic activity, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter through fundamental earnings growth.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero correlation with 60-day forward equity returns, and the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 260, accelerating inflation forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in total nonfarm payrolls and spikes in the unemployment rate confirm a broad-based labor market collapse that destroys consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor deterioration signal yields positive forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts during a zero-interest-rate environment signals renewed confidence in long-term economic stability and construction sector revival, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns when policy rates are near zero, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.
